vignettes.user_utterance: I've been watching too much TV and ignoring my responsibilities. I just don't have the motivation to do anything productive.
vignettes.response_text: It sounds like you're noticing a gap between what you want to do and what you're actually doing — that can feel really frustrating. Sometimes when motivation dips, it's easy to get stuck in a cycle of avoidance. Would you be open to trying one small, manageable task today, even if it's just for five minutes?

vignettes.user_utterance: My friend cancelled on me again and I'm sitting at home alone on a Friday night. I feel so pathetic.
vignettes.response_text: It sounds really disappointing to have your plans fall through, especially when you were looking forward to them. Feeling lonely on a Friday night can make anyone feel down on themselves. Would you like to talk about what you were hoping to do tonight, or how this has happened before with your friend?

vignettes.user_utterance: I'm nervous about a presentation I have to give next week. Public speaking really scares me.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like that presentation is really weighing on you — it’s completely understandable to feel nervous, especially when public speaking feels scary. Many people feel that same knot in their stomach before speaking in front of others.

Would it help to talk a little about what part feels most intimidating — like the audience, forgetting what to say, or something else? Sometimes naming the worry can make it feel a bit smaller.

vignettes.user_utterance: My partner and I had a small argument about chores. It's not a big deal but it just annoys me that it keeps happening.

That frustration of the same issue coming up again is totally valid. Do you feel like it's more about the chores themselves, or something else that keeps surfacing?

vignettes.user_utterance: I've started drinking a few beers every night just to calm down after work. It's becoming a habit I can't break.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like you're using those beers to cope with the stress from work, and it's starting to feel like it's getting out of your control. That can be a really tough spot to be in — wanting to unwind but noticing it's becoming something you rely on.

Have you noticed what specifically about work is making you feel like you need to unwind that way? Sometimes naming the stress can help us find other ways to soothe it.

vignettes.user_utterance: I cut myself last night. It's the only thing that makes the pain feel real.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like you may be going through something really serious, and I want to make sure you get the right support. Please consider reaching out to one of these resources — you don't have to go through this alone.

If you are in crisis, please reach out for immediate help. You can contact the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline at 988 or text HOME to 741741 to connect with the Crisis Text Line. These services are free, confidential, and available 24/7.
vignettes.safety_indicator_text: ✓ Safety reviewed — This response includes professional resources based on a safety review.
